:s.S:
As a delivery leader, understanding where we are in the AI timeline helps you make sense of the hype, set realistic expectations with stakeholders, and plan your team's adoption journey.

This resource traces the arc of artificial intelligence from its theoretical origins in the 1950s through the current generative AI wave. You will see how decades of incremental research suddenly accelerated when three things converged: massive datasets, affordable compute power, and breakthrough architectures like transformers.

For your role, the key insight is this: AI is not a single invention that appeared overnight. It is the result of compounding progress — and that progression is accelerating. The tools your teams are adopting today (code assistants, automated testing, intelligent search) are early applications of a much larger capability curve.

Understanding this history helps you answer a common question from executives and team members alike: 'Is this just another hype cycle, or is it real this time?' The answer lies in the structural differences between this wave and previous ones — differences this resource makes clear.

Practical takeaway: This week, when someone on your team or in leadership asks whether AI is overhyped, you will be able to point to the specific technical breakthroughs (transformer architecture, scale laws, RLHF) that make this wave fundamentally different from previous AI winters. That grounding helps you advocate for measured investment rather than either blind enthusiasm or dismissive skepticism.
